text: *The Record of the Mysterious Center [Xuanzhong Ji] by Guo Pu of the Jin Dynasty* - **Fuxi had a dragon body, and Nüwa had a snake body.** In the annotations to the "Records of the Mysterious Center" by Guo Pu, it is mentioned that the three sons of Zhuanxu all died prematurely and dwelled in human palaces, good at frightening children. - **During the Han dynasty, a ritual was performed using the Five Camps and a Thousand Cavalrymen, carrying torches from the main gate of the imperial palace to send off the plague, and then throwing it into the Luo River.** This ritual is recorded in the "Records of Customs and Festivals of Jingchu" and the "Jade Candle Treasury."

刑天 fought with the emperor as a deity; the emperor cut off his head and buried him on Chángyáng Mountain, then transformed his breasts into eyes and Qí into a mouth. The *Shanhaijing* and *Xuanzhongji* also record this story. Yin Shou made a mirror. The *Yuelu* records this in volume 717, and the *Hailu Sui Shi* in volume 5. Xun Shi made a hat. The *Tongdian* records this in volume 57, and the *Tongzhi Lüe* records it as a hat. The Dog-sealing Clan: Gaoxin had a beautiful woman who was not married. The Xianrong caused chaos, and the emperor said, "Whoever subdues them, I will marry his daughter to him and grant him 300 households." The emperor's dog was named Panhu. In three months, he killed the Xianrong and brought back their heads. The emperor thought it was not suitable to train the people, so he married his daughter to him and sent him to live in exile in the southeast of Kuaiji, 21,000 li away, where he obtained land in the sea, covering an area of 3,000 li. Those born as males became dogs, and those born as females became beautiful women. He was enfeoffed as the ruler of a dog kingdom. The *Leiju* records this in volume 94, and it is also found in *Lushih Fashu* as "the emperor married his daughter to him, and it was not suitable to train the people, so he sent him to live in exile." The last sentence reads, "This is the origin of the Dog-sealing Clan." The Husbandmen: Emperor Taipu of Yin sent Wang Ying to gather herbs from the Queen Mother of the West. Upon reaching this point, they ran out of food and could not proceed, so they ate the fruit of trees and wore tree bark. He remained unmarried for life, and two children were born from his back, and his father died. This is the origin of the husbandmen. They lived 20,000 li from Yumen. The *Yuelu* records this in volume 361.

The People of Fu Fu: A minister of the Yellow Emperor, Xuan Yuan, named Qie Feng, was punished and exiled. He crawled away, and later became known as the People of Fu Fu, living two thousand five hundred li away from the Jade Gate. (Yuelan 797) The Transformed People: They ate leaves for three to seven years, transforming themselves to wrap themselves like silkworms spinning silk, growing wings in nine years, and dying in ten years. They lived four thousand li away from Langya. (Yuelan 825) The Qiheng Clan: Skilled in strange and ingenious crafts, they could build flying vehicles and travel far with the wind. (Yuelan 752) The Land of the Virtuous: An area of a thousand li, abundant with hibiscus flowers. (Leiju 89) The Customs of Yi and Tangwu: The customs of Yi are the same as those of Tangwu, with people living in caves, located ten thousand li away from the Jade Gate. (Shuchao 158) The People of Flying Paths: Living in cold regions, in caves, eating the roots of trees. (Shuchao 158) The People of Ding: Living in cold regions, in caves, eating the meat of birds and mice. They are known as Mingqiu, possibly meaning "famous fur." (Shuchao 158) Zhuwu County: The people serve as laborers, living near the coast. When giving birth, they use sand and stones to support themselves. They do not eat rice, relying solely on fish for sustenance. (Yuelan 361) To the west of Wu, there is the Lake of Juqu, with Mount Bao inside, which has the Sacred Hall of Dongting. This is also mentioned in Chuxue Ji 8. They enter underground and travel to Langya Dongwu. (Huanyu Ji 91, Yuelan 46)

Sichuan has Qingcheng Mountain, which has a cave that leads underground, dividing into three paths, each leading to a different place. Alternatively, it can be read as: "Dividing into three holes, leading northwest to Kunlun." North of Pengcheng, there is Jiu Li Mountain, with a cave that leads to Langya and also to Wangwu, commonly called the Yellow Pool Cave. Water, the most abundant thing in the world, floats in the sky and bears the earth, reaching every high and low place, moistening all things. The strongest thing in the world is the Woji of the East Sea: water flows into it without stopping. Woji is the name of a mountain, located 30,000 li south of the East Sea. Sea water flows into it and immediately disappears, therefore, the water flows southeast without overflowing. The weakest thing in the world is the weak water of Kunlun: even a goose feather cannot float on it. The largest thing in the world is the crab of the North Sea; lifting one claw can make it higher than a mountain, but its body remains in the water.

The greatest in the southeast is the giant turtle: carrying the Mount Penglai on its back, with a circumference of a thousand miles. The *Wenxuan* *Siyuan Fu* annotation in *Yulian* 38 refers to the giant turtle as a giant turtle. *Chuxue Ji* 30 and *Wu Du Fu* annotation in *Wenxuan* refer to the giant turtle as a giant turtle. In the eastern sea, there is a great fish: *Guangji* refers to it as a great fish in the eastern sea. Those who travel the sea will encounter the fish's head for one day and the fish's tail for seven days. Its spawning will turn three hundred miles into blood. *Yulian* 936 and *Guangji* 464 refer to it as producing three days of redness in the green sea. The highest in the world is the branchless tree of Fusang: reaching up to the heavens, coiling and bending downward. *Shilei Fu* annotation 25 refers to it as coiling downward to connect with the three springs. *Qimin Yaoshu* 10 and *Yulian* 955. The largest of the fruit-bearing trees is the peach of Mount Jishan: as large as a ten-ho container. *Qimin Yaoshu* 10, *Leiju* 88, *Chuxue Ji* 28, and *Yulian* 967. In the east is a place called Chaidu, in the state of Qi. *Yulian* 1 refers to these three words, and *Chaidu* is also called *Zhu*. There is a mountain, with a spring on top, like a well, with an unfathomable depth; in spring and summer, it often produces rain and hail. *Yulian* refers to it as producing hail from the well in spring and summer, damaging the five grains. People use firewood to block it, then it does not produce; if not blocked with firewood, it will produce. *Shuchao* 152, *Yulian* 14, and *Guangji* 399.

In the south, there is a volcanic mountain known as the Fire Mountain. According to the *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection), the word "fire" appears in the eastern part of Funan, north of Jia Ying, and west of Zhubo. The mountain starts to emit fire in April and extinguishes it in December. In January, February, and March, the fire does not burn; instead, the mountain only emits clouds, and grass and trees grow leaves and branches. By April, the fire burns, and grass and trees lose their leaves, just like in China during cold weather. Travelers pass by the foot of this mountain in January, February, and March, taking firewood or cutting the wood for firewood, which burns without end. They also take the bark and weave it to make fire-washed cloth. This is recorded in the *Leiji* (Classified Records), volume 80, the *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection), volume 868, and the *Shileifu Zushi* (Annotations on the Categories of Assignments), volume 8. In the north, there is a Bell Mountain. At the top of the mountain, there is a stone that looks like a human head. The left eye is the sun, and the right eye is the moon. Opening the left eye signifies daytime, and opening the right eye signifies nighttime. Opening the mouth represents spring and summer, while closing the mouth represents autumn and winter. This is recorded in the *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection), volume 38. In the southeast, there is a Peach Capital Mountain. At the top of the mountain, there is a large tree named Peach Capital, whose branches are three thousand li apart. There is a heavenly rooster on top. When the sun first rises, its light illuminates this tree, and the heavenly rooster crows, followed by all other chickens. This is recorded in the *Qimin Yaoshu* (Essential Techniques for the Common People), volume 6, the *Leiji* (Classified Records), volume 91, and the *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection), volume 918. There are two deities, the left one named Long and the right one named ○ (unclear). They hold reed ropes to watch for evil spirits and kill them. The above three sentences are added based on the *Yuzhu Baojian* (Treasure Mirror of the Four Seasons) and the map. Today, people make two peach figures stand at the door, placing rooster feathers in the rope, which is a remnant of the past. This is recorded in the *Yuyan* (Imperial Inspection), volume 29, and the *Yuzhu Baojian* (Treasure Mirror of the Four Seasons), volume 1.

To the east of Penglai lies the mountain of Daiyu, upon which stands the tree of Fusang. The tree is ten thousand feet tall. At the top of the tree, there is often a celestial rooster that builds its nest there. Every night at midnight, the celestial rooster crows, and the sun bird in the middle of the day responds to it; when the sun bird crows, all the chickens in the world also crow. In the *Ancient Jade Diagrams* (古玉图谱), Volume Twenty-Four. To the northwest of Kunlun, there is a mountain, which is described in the *Taiping Yulan* (御览) as being to the southwest, circling for thirty thousand li, with a giant snake coiling around it, making three complete turns. The snake is nine thousand li long. Above, the *White Collection* (白帖) Volume Ninety-Eight and the *Taiping Yulan* Volume Thirty-Eight also mention that the snake lives on this mountain, feeding on the vast ocean. In *Leigudao* (类聚) Volume Sixty-Six and *Yiwen Leiju* (广记) Volume Four Hundred Fifty-Six. To the southwest of Yumen Pass is where thunder and lightning occur. In the *Book of Cuts* (书钞), it is mentioned that to the east of the Qiang people's territory, there is a small kingdom with about five or six hundred households, without other duties. There is a mountain within the kingdom, and on top of the mountain, there is a temple. The people of the kingdom annually collect thousands of stones, which they call "Thunder Tips," and offer them to the temple to use for thunder and lightning. The number of stones decreases daily from the spring thunder until they are all gone by autumn. In *Taiping Yulan* Volumes Seven Hundred Ninety-Seven, Thirteen, and Fifty-Two, and *Book of Cuts* Volume One Hundred Fifty-Two, and *Feng Shi Wen Jian Ji* (封氏闻见记) Volume Eight, and *Shi Lei Fu Zhu* (事类赋注) Volume Seven. In the East Sea, there is a dangerous place called Snake Hill, where there is much wet and marshy ground, and many snakes live, but no people. Some of the snakes have human heads and snake bodies. In *Leigudao* Volume Sixty-Six and *Yiwen Leiju* Volume Four Hundred Fifty-Six. On the mountain of Yuanqiu, there are many large snakes, which are suppressed with male yellow essence (雄黄精). In *Taiping Yulan* Volume Nine Hundred Eighty-Eight.

The Great Yuezhi and the Western Hu, a three-character term mentioned in the *Shu Chao* (Book of Collections) as "日反" (or variations such as "日支牛," "反牛," "日及," and "白皮牛"), which likely refers to a type of ox or a cultural concept. Today, one cuts and takes its flesh, three or four pounds (or two or three pounds, according to *Lei Ju*), and the next day, the flesh has already returned, and the wound heals immediately (or the wound heals the next day, according to *Shi Lei Fu Zhi* and *Tong Dian*). The *Huan Yu Ji* (Record of the World) also mentions that it is called "复牛" (or "复作及," a variant or error in the text), meaning "cut and regrows." When Han people entered this country, they did not know that the ox was considered a treasure. Han people said, "We have a creature, as big as a finger, called a silkworm, which eats mulberry leaves and spits out silk for humans." Foreigners no longer believe in the existence of silkworms. In the West of the Great Tree Mountain, there is a tree that bears flowers. By consuming its fruit, one can understand the languages of all nations. In the north of Xuantu, there is a mountain with flowers, and people pick them to spin and weave into cloth. East of the Eastern Sea, there is a tree named Bai Meng, whose juice can be made into fat, white like fat, and sweet in taste. In Jingzhou, there is a tree named Wujiao, whose fruit is like sesame seeds, and its juice is like fat, with a taste similar to pork fat. According to *Qi Min Yao Shu* (Essential Techniques for the People's Welfare), in Jingyang, the fruit is like a chicken head, and when pressed, it is like sesame seeds, and its juice tastes like pork fat.

When a zelkova tree is used as a pillar at the bottom, the tree makes a sound, which is said to be a struggle for position. (Source: *Yunfu* 8 Geng) A thousand-year-old tree has branches in the center that are lower and those on the sides that are higher. (Source: *Yuelan* 952) A hundred-year-old tree has sap that is red like blood. (Sources: *Leiju* 88, *Zhulin* 28, *Yuelan* 952) The spirit of a thousand-year-old tree becomes a green sheep, and the spirit of a ten-thousand-year-old tree becomes a green ox, often traveling among humans. (Sources: *Leiju* 94, *Yuelan* 901, *Chuxueji* 29, *Baitie* 96, *Zhulin* 28, *Leiju* 88, *Yuelan* 886, *Yuelan* 900) During the reign of Emperor Huan of Han, while he was touring the river, suddenly a green ox emerged from the river and ran straight towards Emperor Huan, causing everyone to run away. (Source: *Yuelan* 239, *Yuelan* 900) At that time, General He of the Grand Council (太尉何公) was a high-ranking official in the imperial court, known for his bravery and strength. He ran to meet the ox. The ox saw the official and turned back to the river. Before reaching the river, the official caught up with the ox and pulled out its left foot with his left hand, then chopped off its head with his right hand, killing it. This green ox was the spirit of a ten-thousand-year-old tree. (Sources: *Shuchao* 64, *Yuelan* 239, *Yuelan* 900) According to the official, his account is somewhat brief, so the current version follows *Yuelan*. (Source: *Yuelan* 900)

Emperor Qin Wen Gong constructed the Chang'an Palace, which extended to cover an area of 400 li, reaching south to Mount Zhongnan. On the mountain, there was a massive梓tree, several hundred围in circumference, providing shade to the palace. The ruler disliked the tree and attempted to cut it down, but for several days, he was unsuccessful. According to *Yulian*, during the reign of Qin Shi Huang, the king was displeased and sent troops to cut down the tree, causing a great storm with flying sand and stones, and people fled in panic; by night, the wounds had healed. One person, caught in the storm, was injured and could not move. According to *Zhu Lin*, the person was cold and stayed for the night. At night, he heard a ghost come to question the tree, saying, "The Qin King is cruel and violent, attacking you, are you not in distress?" The tree replied, "If you come, I will make the wind and rain strike you, what can you do to me?" The ghost then said, "If the Qin King sends 300 people, with red silk ropes around the tree, to cut you down, will you not be defeated?" The tree remained silent. According to *Yulian*, the tree said nothing, and according to *Zhu Lin*, the tree was silent. The next day, someone reported to the king, and the king followed this advice to cut down the tree. According to *Yulian*, the person rushed in to report to Qin Shi Huang, and according to *Zhu Lin*, the person reported to the king, and the tree was cut down, revealing a mythical青牛that fled in terror; it was chased into the Li River. According to *Shu Chao* and *Zhu Lin*, a青牛appeared to greet them and entered the river. According to *Yulian*, a青牛appeared in the center and was chased into the water. According to *Shi Lei Fu Zhu*, the king then established a guard of mounted soldiers.

The Ghostly Bird of Yu Zhang The Ghostly Bird of Yu Zhang flies at night and hides during the day, and is classified as a spirit or ghost. When it has feathers, it appears as a flying bird; when it sheds its feathers, it transforms into a woman. According to the *Bei Hu Lu*, this description is placed above the story of the man from Yu Zhang. It is also known by several names: "Daughter of Heaven," "Night-Walking Maiden," "Hook Star," and "Hidden Flyer." The bird does not have its own offspring and often adopts human children, raising them as its own. Today, people do not leave children's clothes exposed at night because this creature marks them with blood to identify them, and then takes the child.

In modern times, as recorded in the *Jingchu Suishiji* with a note, there are families with young children who mark their clothes with blood to ward off evil. The *Yuelan* (Imperial Reader) records that people who raise young children should not expose their clothes, as this bird will take the child. The *Jingchu Suishiji* note also mentions that this is more common in Jingzhou. In the past, a man from Yuzhang saw six or seven women in a field, not knowing they were birds, he crawled towards them and first obtained one of their feathered coats. He took it and kept it, then approached the other birds. The other birds each went to their feathered coats, put them on, and flew away. One bird alone could not leave, so the man took her as his wife. They had three daughters. Later, the mother sent one of the daughters to ask the father, and she found out that the coat was under a pile of rice. She got it, put it on, and flew away. Later, she brought the coat to welcome her three daughters, and the three daughters, after getting the coat, also flew away. Now it is called the "ghost carriage." The *Yuelan* records this in volumes 883 and 927, and also in volume 13 of the *Beihu Lu*. The *Shuijing Zhu* (Commentary on the Waterways Classic) records that a man from Yangxin found one of these birds by the water and lived with it, having two daughters who all flew away wearing feathers. In Yuzhang, people do not expose their children's clothes, saying that if the bird's dust falls on a child's clothes, it will make the child sick, thus it is also called the "night-flying maiden." Zhu Yi's *Yiji Liao Zaji* (Miscellaneous Notes from the Yiji Studio) cites this in the same way as the *Shuijing Zhu*.

Foxes and Other Creatures A fox at fifty years old can transform into a woman. At a hundred years old, it becomes a beautiful woman; the *Initial Studies Record* (卷二十九) also cites the first sentence as stating that a thousand-year-old fox becomes a promiscuous woman and a divine witch; the *Imperial Reader* (卷九百九十) also cites the first sentence as stating that a fifty-year-old fox becomes a promiscuous woman and the last sentence as stating that it also becomes a divine witch or a man, interacting with women; it can know things happening a thousand miles away; it is skilled in witchcraft and can make people confused and lose their minds. At a thousand years old, it communicates with heaven and becomes a heavenly fox. *Comprehensive Records* (卷四百四十七) A hundred-year-old mouse transforms into a deity. *Imperial Reader* (卷九百十一) A hundred-year-old mouse transforms into a bat. *Initial Studies Record* (卷二十九), *White Paper* (卷九十八), *Imperial Reader* (卷九百十一) A hundred-year-old bat, its color is red, and it hangs upside down when resting; if one obtains and consumes it, it can make one a deity. *Water Classic Annotations* (卷二十七) cites this. A thousand-year-old bat, its color is white; if one eats it, one will live ten thousand years. *Classified Collections* (卷九十七), *Imperial Reader* (卷九百四十六) A thousand-year-old crane, it calls out according to the time. *Tang Manuscript of the *Classified Collections* found in the Dunhuang Cave* A thousand-year-old swallow, it faces north when perched. *Classified Collections* (卷九十二), *Miscellaneous Records of the West and East* (续八) A thousand-year-old turtle, it can speak with humans. *Spring and Autumn Annals: Commentary on the Year of Duke Wen, Fourth Year* (正义) cites *Xuanzhong Yaoji* A thousand-year-old turtle, it can speak with humans. *Initial Studies Record* (卷三十) A thousand-year-old toad, it has horns on its head; if one obtains and eats it, one will live a thousand years. The *Precious Canon of the Jade Candle* (卷五), *Comprehensive Rhyme* (卷二十四盐) also cite this; it can also consume mountain spirits. *Imperial Reader* (卷九百四十九)

The Mountain Spirit resembles a human; it has one foot that is three to four feet long, eats mountain crabs, appears at night and hides during the day, and humans cannot see it but can hear its sound. A thousand-year-old toad eats it. (Taiping Guangji 816, Cao Tang Shi Jian 3) The Yù is three to four inches long, and toads, phoenixes, and mandarin ducks all eat it. (Guangyun 25 de) The Shui Hu, when viewed in shape, resembles an insect, and its breath is that of a ghost. It is three to four inches long, black in color, about an inch wide, with a shell on its back that is about three inches thick. It has something on its head that looks like a horn, and when it sees a person, it shoots a gas at them. It can shoot a gas at a person from two to three steps away; when hit, out of ten people, six to seven die. (Taiping Guangji 950, Guangji 473, Xuanzhong Ji mentions that the Yù shoots a gas at a person from thirty steps away and hits their shadow, killing sixteen or seventeen out of twenty people. Jing Shi Zheng Lei Ben Cao 22 mentions that the Shui Hu is a creature that is three inches long, black in color, with a shell on its back, and a horn on its head that shoots a gas at people. In the Jianghuai region, it is called the Duan Hu and is known as the She Gong, and is associated with Xi Bing. Because it is a creature, it can suppress other creatures.) The Yue Yan has a speckled chest and a small voice; the Hu Yan has a red collar and a loud voice. (Dan Qian Zong Lu) The essence of jade becomes a white tiger. The essence of gold becomes a carriage and horses. The essence of copper becomes servants. (Taiping Guangji 813 mentions that the essence of copper becomes slaves. Taiping Guangji 886 mentions that the essence of lead becomes an old woman. Guangyun 23 xi mentions that the essence of lead and tin becomes a maid. Taiping Guangji 812 mentions that the essence of lead becomes an old maid. Taiping Guangji 912 mentions that the essence of lead becomes a fox.)

Pine resin, when seeped into the earth, transforms into *fu ling* (poria cocos) and *fu shen* (a type of fungus) after a thousand years. *Chu xue ji* 28, *lei ju* 88, *guang yun* 953, *shi lei fu zhu* 24, with the final two characters. Maple resin, when seeped into the earth, becomes *hu bo* (amber) after a thousand years. *Yu luan* 808, *you yang za zu* 11, citing that maple resin becomes amber when it enters the earth. Coral originates from the western sea of Daqin (Roman Empire), growing on stones underwater. It is initially white, turns yellow after one year, red after three years, and is consumed by insects and decays after four years. *Yu luan* 807. Diamond comes from Tianzhu (India) in Daqin (Roman Empire), also known as *xue yu dao* (diamond knife). It can cut jade as easily as an iron knife cuts wood. Large ones are about a foot long, while small ones are the size of rice grains. When carving jade, one should use a large gold ring, place it on the finger, open the back like a crescent moon, and insert the jade-cutting knife into the ring to carve the jade. *Yu luan* 813. Gold rings come from Tianzhu (India) in Daqin (Roman Empire). *Shu chao* 136. Agate comes from Dàyuezhī. *Tong dian* 192, *lei ju* 84, *yu luan* 808. *Che qu* (a type of shell) comes from Tianzhu (India). *Lei ju* 84, *yu luan* 808. Daqin (Roman Empire) has five-colored *po li* (glass); red is the most valuable. *Yu luan* 808. *Mù nán* (a type of gemstone) comes from Daqin (Roman Empire). *Yu luan* 809. Five meats and seven vegetables are preferable to preserved and strong-smelling foods. *Shu chao* 145.
《玄中记》[晋]郭璞 伏牺龙身，女娲蛇躯。文选鲁灵光殿赋注路史后纪二注 路史后纪一又引首句 颛顼氏三子俱亡，处人宫室，善惊小儿。汉世以五营千骑，自端门传炬送疫，弃洛水中。荆楚岁时记注玉烛宝典十二引作自端门送至洛水

刑天与帝争神；帝断其首，葬之常羊山，乃以乳为目，以齐为口。御览五百五十引山海经注云玄中记亦载 尹寿作镜。御览七百十七 海录碎事五 旬始作冠。通典五十七 通志略引冠作帽 狗封氏者：高辛氏有美女，未嫁。犬戎为乱，帝曰，有讨之者，妻以美女，封三百户。帝之狗名槃护，御览引作槃瓠三月而杀犬戎，以其首来。帝以为不可训民，乃妻以女流之，会稽东南二万一千里，得海中土。方三千里，御览引千作百而封之，生男为狗，生女为美女。类聚九十四封为狗民国。御览九百五 亦见路史发挥二引帝以二句作帝以女妻之不可教训流作浮美女作美人末句作是为犬封氏 丈夫民：殷帝太戊，使王英采药于西王母。至此绝粮，不能进，乃食木实，衣以木皮。终身无妻，产子二人，从背?间出其父则死。是为丈夫民。去玉门二万里。御览三百六十一

扶伏民者：黄帝轩辕之臣曰茄丰，有罪，刑而放之，扶伏而去，后是为扶伏民，去玉门开二万五千里。御览七百九十七 化民，食叶三七年化，能以自裹如蚕绩，九年生翼，十年而死。去琅邪四万里。御览八百二十五 奇肱氏，善奇巧，能为飞车，从风远行。御览七百五十二 君子之国，地方千里，多木槿之华。类聚八十九 伊俗与唐吾同俗，民穴居，去玉门一万里。书钞一百五十八 飞路之民，地寒，穴居，食木根。书钞一百五十八 丁○之民，地寒，穴居，食禽鼠之肉，民号为名裘。书钞一百五十八 朱梧县：其民服役，依海际居。产子，以沙石自拥。不食米，正资鱼以为生气。御览三百六十一 吴国西有具区泽，中有包山，山有洞庭宝室。已上亦见初学记八入地下潜行，通琅琊东武。寰宇记九十一御览四十六

蜀郡有青城山，有洞穴一引作青城有穴潜行，分道为三，道各通一处，已上二句一引作分为三孔西北通昆仑。御览五十四又一百六十六 彭城北有九里山，有穴潜通琅琊，又通王屋，俗呼为黄池穴。寰宇记十五书钞一百五十八白帖六引并作彭城北有黄池穴如洞室北通王屋山 天下之多者水也，事类赋注引作莫水若也浮天载地：高下无所不至，万物无所不润。水经注序类聚八初学记六白帖六御览五十九文选海赋注引前二句 天下之强选注引作大者，东海之沃焦焉：白帖引作恶蕉水灌之而不已。沃焦者，山名也，在东海南方三万里，海水灌之而即消，故水东南流而不盈也。御览五十二又六十寰宇记二十二类聚八白帖六文选江赋注事类赋注六 天下之弱者，有昆仑之弱水焉：鸿毛不能起也。史记匈奴传索隐汉书外夷传注御览六十五 草堂诗笺十 天下之大物，北海之蟹；举一螯能加于山，身故在水中。御览九百四十二

东南之大者，巨鳌焉：以背负蓬莱山，周回千里。文选思玄赋注御览三十八巨鳌，巨龟也。初学记三十文选吴都赋注引云巨鳌龟也 东方之东海，有大鱼焉：广记引作东方之大者东海鱼焉行海者一日逢鱼头，七日逢鱼尾，其产则三百里为血。御览九百三十六广记四百六十四成玄英庄子逍遥游疏引作产三日碧海为之变红 天下之高者，有扶桑无枝木焉：上至于天，盘蜿而下屈，事类赋注二十五引作盘屈而下通三泉。齐民要术十御览九百五十五 木子之大者，有积石山之桃实焉：大如十斛笼。齐民要术十类聚八十八初学记二十八御览九百六十七 东方有柴都焉，在齐国。御览一引有此三字又都作渚有山，山上有泉，如井状，深不测；春夏常出雨雹，御览引作至春夏时雹从井中出败五谷。人以柴木塞之，则不出；不柴塞，则出也；已上九字御览引有故曰柴都焉书钞一百五十二御览十四又七十广记三百九十九

南方有炎火山焉。御览引有火字在扶南国之东，加营国之北，诸薄国之西。山从四月而火生；十二月火灭；正月二月三月火不然，山上但出云气，而草木生叶枝条；至四月火然，草木叶落，如中国寒时草木叶落也。行人以正月二月三月行过此山下，取柴以御览引作取此木为薪，然之无尽时；取其皮绩之，以为火浣布。类聚八十御览八百六十八事类赋注八 北方有钟山焉，山上有石首如人首：左目为日，右目为月；开左目为昼，开右目为夜；开口为春夏，闭口为秋冬。御览三十八 东南有桃都山，上有大树，名曰桃都，枝相去三千里。上有一天鸡，日初出，光照此木，天鸡则鸣，群鸡皆随之鸣。齐民要术六类聚九十一御览九百十八下有二神，左名隆，右名○，玉烛宝典一注并执苇索伺不祥之鬼，得而煞之。已上三句以玉烛宝典引括地图补今人正朝作两桃人立门旁，以雄鸡毛置索中，盖遗象也。御览二十九玉烛宝典一

蓬莱之东，岱舆之山，上有扶桑之树。树高万丈。树巅常有天鸡，为巢于上。每夜至子时，则天鸡鸣，而日中阳乌应之；阳乌鸣，则天下之鸡皆鸣。古玉图谱二十四 昆仑西北有山，御览引作西南山周回三万里，巨蛇绕之，得三周。蛇为长九万里。已上白帖九十八御览三十八亦引蛇居此山，饮食沧海。类聚九十六广记四百五十六 玉门之西南为霹雳。三字书钞引有羌之东，有一国，五六百户，无他事役。国中有山，山上有祠庙。国人每岁出尖一引作石?事类赋注同小注云一作砧书钞引作?闻见记引作礸数千枚，输于庙中，名霹雳尖，以给霹雳所用。从春雷出而尖日减，至秋尖尽。御览七百九十七又十三又五十二书钞一百五十二封氏闻见记八事类赋注七 东海有蛇丘之地险，多渐洳，众蛇居之，无人民。蛇或人头而蛇身。类聚九十六广记四百五十六 员丘之上多大蛇，以雄黄精压之。御览九百八十八

大月氏及西胡，三字书钞引有有牛名为日反：书钞百四十五引作日支牛御览九百引作反牛通典一百九十二引作日及事类赋注二十二引作白皮牛今日割取其肉三四斤，类聚引作二三斤明日其肉已复，创即愈也。类聚引作明日疮愈事类赋注通典引并同御览九百又一百六十六引云割而复生名曰复牛寰宇记八十同复作及汉人入此国，见牛不知以为珍异。汉人曰：“吾国有虫，大小如指，名为蚕，食桑叶，为人吐丝。”外国人不复信有蚕也。类聚六十五御览八百二十五 大树之山，西有采华之树，服之则通万国之言。类聚八十八御览九百五十二 玄菟北有山，山有花，人取纺织为布。御览八百二十 东海之东，有树名为白蒙，其汁可为脂，色白如脂，味甘。书钞一百四十七 荆州有树名乌臼，实如胡麻子，其汁如脂，其味亦如猪脂味也。书钞一百四十七齐民要术十引作荆阳有乌臼其实如鸡头迮之如胡麻子其汁味如猪脂

凡梓木为楹居下，则木鸣，谓之争位。韵府八庚 千岁之树：枝中央下，四边高。御览九百五十二百岁之树：其汁赤如血。类聚八十八珠林二十八御览九百五十二 千岁树精为青羊，万岁树精为青牛，多出游人间。类聚九十四御览九百一引首句初学记二十九白帖九十六引次句珠林二十八类聚八十八引前二句御览八百八十六引全 汉桓帝时，出游河上，忽有一青牛从河中出，直走荡桓帝边，人皆惊走；御览引有此句太尉何公时为殿中将军，御览一引作中尉将军为人勇力，走往逆之。牛见公往，乃反走还河。未至河，公及牛，乃以手拔牛左足脱，以右手持斧斫牛头而杀之。此青牛是万年木精也。书钞六十四御览二百三十九又九百 牛见公往已下书钞引作手揽其右足牛见公乃走还河案何公进其文颇略今从御览

秦文公造长安宫，面四百里，南至终南山。山有梓树，御览一引作秦始皇时终南山有梓树珠林引同大数百围，荫宫中。公恶而伐之，连日不克。御览一引作始皇恶之兴兵伐之珠林同天辄大风雨，飞沙石，人皆疾走；至夜疮合。有一人，中风雨，伤蹇不能去。蹇御览作寒依珠林引改留宿。夜闻有鬼来问树，言秦王凶暴相伐，得不困耶？树曰；“来即作风雨击之，其奈吾何。”鬼又曰；“秦王若使三百人，被头，以赤丝绕树伐汝，得无败乎？”树默然不应。御览一引作树淡然无言珠林引谈作寞明日，人上言；秦王依此言伐之。御览一引作疾入报秦皇案言伐珠林引作病人报秦案言伐之树断，中有青牛骇逸；逐之入澧水。书钞珠林引并作有一青牛出迎之走入河御览一引作中央有一青牛出逐之入水今依事类赋注秦王因立旄头骑。御览六百八十又九百五十八珠林六十七书钞一百三十事类赋注二十四
姑获鸟夜飞昼藏，盖鬼神类。衣毛为飞鸟，脱毛为女人。二句北户录引在豫章男子句上一名天帝少女，一名夜行游女，御览引作名曰帝少女一名夜游今依北户录引补一名钩星，御览一引作钓星一名隐飞。鸟无子，喜取人子养之，以为子。今时小儿之衣不欲夜露者，为此物爱以血点其衣为志，即取小儿也。

今时至此已上荆楚岁时记注引作有小儿之家即以血点其衣为志御览引作人养小儿不可露其衣此鸟度即取儿也经史证类本草十九引作今时人小儿衣不欲夜露者为此也今依北户录引补故世人名为鬼鸟，荆楚岁时记注引有此句荆州为多。昔豫章男子，见田中有六七女人，不知是鸟，匍匐往，先得其毛衣，取藏之，即往就诸鸟。诸鸟各去就毛衣，衣之飞去。一鸟独不得去，男子取以为妇。生三女。其母后使女问父，知衣在积稻下，得之，衣而飞去。后以衣迎三女，三女儿得衣亦飞去。今谓之鬼车。御览八百八十三又九百二十七有末句又十三北户录一 水经注三十五引云阳新男子于水次得之遂与共居生二女悉衣羽而去豫章间养儿不露其衣言是鸟落尘于儿衣中则令儿病故亦谓之夜飞游女矣 朱翌猗觉寮杂记下引与水经注同

狐五十岁能变化为妇人。百岁为美女；二句初学记二十九亦引首句作千岁之狐为淫妇为神巫；三句御览九百九十亦引首句作五十岁之狐为淫妇末句作又为巫神或为丈夫，与女人交接；能知千里外事；善蛊魅，使人迷惑失智。千岁即与天通为天狐。广记四百四十七 百岁鼠化为神。御览九百十一 百岁之鼠，化为蝙蝠。初学记二十九白帖九十八御览九百十一 百岁伏翼，其色赤，止则倒县；得而服之，使人神仙。二句依水经注二十七引补 千岁伏翼，色白；得食之，寿万岁。类聚九十七御览九百四十六 千岁之鹤，随时鸣。敦煌石室所出唐写本类书残卷 千岁之燕，户北向。类聚九十二酉阳杂俎续八 千岁之鼋，能与人语。春秋左传文四年正义引玄中要记 千岁之龟，能与人语。初学记三十 千岁蟾蜍，头生角；得而食之，寿千岁。已上二句玉烛宝典五广韵二十四盐亦引又能食山精。御览九百四十九

山精如人；一足长三四尺，食山蟹，夜出昼藏，人不能见，夜闻其声；千岁蟾蜍食之。御览八百十六 草堂诗笺三 蜮长三四寸，蟾蜍，鸑鷟鸳鸯悉食之。广韵二十五德 水狐者，视其形虫也，其气乃鬼也。长三四寸。其色黑。广寸许。背上有甲，厚三分许。其头有物，向前如角状。见人则气射人。去二三步即射人；中，十人六七人死。御览九百五十 广记四百七十三题感应经引玄中记云蜮以气射人去人三十步即射中其影中人死十六七经史证类本草二十二引云水狐虫也长四寸其色黑背上有甲其口有角向前如弩以气射人江淮间谓之短狐射工通为溪病此既其虫故能相压伏也 越燕，斑胸，声小；胡燕，红襟，声大。丹铅总录 玉精为白虎。金精为车马。铜精为僮奴。御览八百十三引作奴婢铅精为老妇。御览八百八十六 广韵二十三锡引云铅锡之精为婢御览八百十二引作为老婢又九百十二引作为狐狸

松脂沦入地中，千岁为茯苓，伏神。初学记二十八类聚八十八广韵御览九百五十三事类赋注二十四有末二字 枫脂沦入地中，千秋为虎珀。御览八百八酉阳杂俎十一引云枫脂入地为琥珀 珊瑚出大秦国西海中，生水中石上。初生白，一年黄，三年赤，四年虫食败。御览八百七 金钢出天竺大秦国，一名削玉刀。削玉如铁刀削木。大者长尺许，小者如稻米。欲刻玉时，当作大金镮，着手指间，开其背如月，以割玉刀内镮中，以刻玉。御览八百十三 天竺大秦国出金指镮。书钞一百三十六 马瑙出大月氐。通典一百九十二类聚八十四御览八百八 车渠出天竺国。类聚八十四御览八百八 大秦国有五色颇黎；红色最贵。御览八百八 木难出大秦。御览八百九 五肉七菜，胜腌腥臊。书钞一百四十五
